mapping.a_element: discrete point set of ~92M halo positions sampling the cosmic density field
mapping.b_element: discrete set of local measurements/projections recorded along the observer's worldline

mapping.a_element: Delaunay tessellation assembling the sampled points into a simplicial complex
mapping.b_element: simplicial complex assembled out of the recorded measurements

mapping.a_element: topology extracted by DisPerSE from the complex (filament network, density maxima/minima, halo connectivity)
mapping.b_element: topological information about spacetime read off from the complex

mapping.a_element: preserving correct global topology despite block-decomposition and boundary artifacts
mapping.b_element: capturing topology even when the spacetime geometry is fluctuating

brief_justification: Both recover the topology of an underlying space from a discrete set of local samples by assembling those samples into a simplicial complex and reading off its topological invariants: DisPerSE's Delaunay tessellation of halo positions plays the same role as the observer's worldline-measurement complex, and both target a topology robust to perturbation (tiling boundaries vs. spacetime fluctuations). The concrete build rules differ (empty-circumsphere geometry vs. von Neumann-algebra projections), but the transferable machinery -- simplicial-complex reconstruction of a space's topology from local data -- is shared.
